title: Privilege Escalation - Local Admin Token Abuse via Token Impersonation
mitre_technique: T1134.001 - Access Token Manipulation: Token Impersonation/Theft
raw_evidence: 2025-07-14T14:03:58Z — EDR alert: process ImpersonateLoggedOnUser called by devtools_helper.exe (unsigned, dropped to C:\Windows\Temp 4 minutes prior).
2025-07-14T14:04:01Z — devtools_helper.exe duplicated access token of svc_deploy (a domain service account with local admin on 14 servers).
2025-07-14T14:04:03Z — New process spawned under svc_deploy token: cmd.exe — executed net localgroup administrators /domain.
2025-07-14T14:04:11Z — net user /domain executed — full domain user enumeration.
2025-07-14T14:04:19Z — Attempted WMI remote execution to SRV-PROD-001 using svc_deploy token — blocked by host firewall.
2025-07-14T14:04:44Z — devtools_helper.exe origin: downloaded via curl from 194.147.78.33 by user r.okonkwo 6 minutes before alert.
2025-07-14T14:05:02Z — r.okonkwo is a junior developer with no legitimate reason to access svc_deploy or run token manipulation tools.

context.svc_deploy_scope: local admin on 14 production and staging servers
context.asset_criticality_destination: HIGH — developer server with lateral path to production
